name: Qwen3.5-397B-A17B
archDisplay: Hybrid-attention MoE (Qwen3-Next lineage): 60 layers, 15x(3x(Gated DeltaNet -> MoE) -> 1x(Gated Attention -> MoE)); 512 experts, 10 routed + 1 shared activated per token; hidden dim 4096; natively multimodal ("Causal Language Model with Vision Encoder" per HF card, not text-only)
note: Open-weight flagship of the Qwen3.5 generation; native vision-language model (not a bolt-on VL variant) built on Qwen3-Next-style hybrid linear-attention/MoE architecture. Native 262,144-token context, extensible to ~1.01M via YaRN. First confirmed model of the flagged 'missing generation'.

results.notes: SWE-bench Verified. Corrected from 76.2 to 76.4: the model's own HF model card benchmark table AND HF's separate structured 'Evaluation results' widget both show 76.4, matching the original qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen3.5 launch blog. The candidate's cited value (76.2) came from the later qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen3.6-27b cross-generation table, which used a different/refined agent scaffold for its comparisons -- it is not the model's canonical self-reported figure, and the original record's claim that 76.2 is 'corroborated verbatim on the HF model card' is factually incorrect (the HF card shows 76.4).
GPQA Diamond. Verified correct via fresh fetch of HF model card table and the original qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen3.5 launch blog table (both show 88.4); also matches automatio.ai third-party listing.
Verified correct via fresh fetch of HF model card table, qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen3.5 launch blog table, and Ollama registry readme (all show 87.8).
LiveCodeBench v6. Verified correct via fresh fetch of HF model card table and qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen3.5 launch blog table (both show 83.6).

Humanity's Last Exam, text-only setting. Verified correct via fresh fetch of HF model card table and qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen3.5 launch blog table (both show 28.7); also matches automatio.ai third-party listing.

AIME26 (AIME 2026). Corrected from 93.3 to 91.3: the model's own HF model card table and the original qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen3.5 launch blog both report 91.3, corroborated independently by third-party trackers automatio.ai and morphllm.com. The candidate's cited value (93.3) came from qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen3.6-27b's cross-generation table, which carries an explicit footnote from Qwen themselves: 'We use the full AIME 2026 (I & II), where the scores may differ from Qwen 3.5 notes' -- i.e. Qwen is flagging that this is a different, non-canonical re-evaluation, not the model's own self-reported figure.

GDPval-AA v2 ('agentic real-world work tasks', normalized as (Elo-500)/2000), independently measured by Artificial Analysis on the model's live AA page as part of Intelligence Index v4.1 -- not currently tracked in the file (file only has self-reported osworld-verified for the agentic/visual-agent category). Qwen3.5 397B A17B scored 23%, well behind Claude Fable 5 (63%) and GPT-5.6 Sol (62%) but ahead of Ring-2.6-1T (21%) and DeepSeek V3.1 Terminus (19%). [SCALE FIX 2026-07-17: corrected from a normalized percentage (23%) to this taxonomy's actual raw 0-2000-point Elo scale (matching the majority convention used by e.g. Gemini 3.1 Pro=1317, Claude Fable 5=1932). Converted via Artificial Analysis's own documented formula, normalized=(Elo-500)/2000, i.e. raw=23*20+500=960. This formula is independently cross-validated against Kimi K2.7 Code's directly-disclosed embedded-JSON raw Elo (1186.79 for a displayed 34%, matching the formula almost exactly).]

LMArena (Chatbot Arena, now rebranded arena.ai) Text Arena Overall leaderboard: qwen3.5-397b-a17b listed under Alibaba/Apache 2.0, Elo score 1442 (+/-4), rank 65 of 374 models, 55,130 votes, snapshot dated Jul 13, 2026. Not previously tracked -- the file has no blind-human-preference arena result at all, only self-reported benchmark-suite scores.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real and independently corroborated. Primary sources fetched directly: HF model card (huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3.5-397B-A17B, full spec + benchmark table), the QwenLM GitHub org (github.com/QwenLM/Qwen3.5 redirects to github.com/QwenLM/Qwen3.6, whose README changelog gives the official release date 2026-02-16 and confirms Apache-2.0 licensing for all open-weight models in the family), and the qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen3.5 launch post. Press corroboration: CNBC (the exact lead URL, cnbc.com/2026/02/17/...) confirms the Feb 2026 launch and notes CNBC could not independently verify Alibaba's self-reported benchmark comparisons -- flagged accordingly (all results here are self-reported). GPQA Diamond score (88.4) is corroborated twice within the same source (blog table + HF's own embedded community leaderboard widget for that exact dataset). Architecture is hybrid (Gated DeltaNet linear-attention blocks interleaved with regular Gated Attention blocks, MoE FFN) -- this is the Qwen3-Next architectural lineage, not a plain MoE like Qwen3-235B-A22B, so archClass is set to 'hybrid' rather than 'moe'. Native context is 262,144 tokens (=256K), extensible to 1,010,000 via documented YaRN scaling -- ctxK set to the native figure per corpus convention (matches how Qwen3-Coder-480B-A35B records native rather than YaRN-extended context). No official Alibaba Cloud Model Studio API price found for the raw open-weight model id in this pass (only the hosted 'Plus' product has listed pricing); apiAvailable is true based on third-party inference listings (OpenRouter etc.) referenced across secondary sources.

[2026-07-16] Researched the three missing core benchmarks (mmlu, math, humaneval); concluded all three are legitimate absences, not gaps in extraction effort. (1) Official source: fresh fetch of the model's own HF model card (huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3.5-397B-A17B) enumerated every row in both the Language and Vision-Language benchmark tables -- plain MMLU, plain MATH, and HumanEval do not appear anywhere; Qwen replaced them for this generation with MMLU-Redux/MMLU-Pro (knowledge), MathVision/MathVista (vision-math; no text-only MATH variant reported), and LiveCodeBench v6/SWE-bench Verified (coding). (2) Independent leaderboards: Artificial Analysis's live model page (artificialanalysis.ai/models/qwen3-5-397b-a17b, fetched fresh) lists 18 tracked benchmarks for this model and none of them are MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval; Epoch AI has no dedicated model page for this exact model (epoch.ai/models/qwen3.5-397b-a17b returns 404, only an aggregate 'Qwen 3.5 Plus' ECI-score entry exists with no sub-benchmark breakdown); Vals.ai has no page for this model. (3) Sibling cross-reference: this corpus's own qwen3-5-122b-a10b.json record documents a prior agent's line-by-line grep of the full ~70-row HF README for that same-generation sibling, which explicitly confirms AIME/GSM8K/MATH are 'notably absent' from the vendor's own card; qwen3-6-27b.json (the successor dense flagship, same qwen.ai/blog and HF-card sourcing pattern) likewise has no mmlu/math/humaneval entries. Third-party SEO aggregators (automatio.ai, llm-stats.com) do surface numbers labeled MMLU (88.4) and HumanEval (79.3/76.4) with no source citation, but automatio.ai's 'MMLU' value is identical to this model's already-verified GPQA score (88.4) and a second aggregator independently mislabels MMLU-Pro as 88.4 too (vs. the verified 87.8) -- a telltale field-copy/scraper artifact, not a genuine independent measurement, so these were disregarded per the corpus's no-guessing rule. Conclusion: Qwen deliberately did not evaluate/publish MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval for the Qwen3.5 generation, favoring harder successor benchmarks instead -- a legitimate 'saturated legacy benchmark' omission, all three marked omitted_no_evidence.

[2026-07-17] Second, deeper research pass specifically hunting for mmlu/math/humaneval via source types NOT used in the first pass (which had exhausted official launch materials, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena, BenchLM, and llm-stats.com). Checked all 6 prescribed new source types; none yielded a credible citable score for any of the three benchmarks.

(1) Stanford HELM: Attempted direct fetch of crfm.stanford.edu/helm/lite/latest/ and /helm/mmlu/latest/ (JS-rendered SPA, no static model list retrievable) plus targeted web searches for "Qwen3.5" across crfm.stanford.edu. No evidence found that HELM (Classic, Lite, Capabilities, or the Arabic-language leaderboard) has ever run this model -- the most recent Qwen coverage found on any HELM leaderboard is Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507 (an older, prior-generation model), confirming HELM has not caught up to Qwen3.5-397B-A17B (released 2026-02-16).

(2) arXiv/Semantic Scholar baselines: Found and fetched two papers that use this exact model as a baseline/teacher: (a) "Compress-Distill" (arxiv.org/html/2606.05988v1) uses Qwen3.5-397B-A17B as a distillation teacher and reports only per-dataset trace-generation accuracy (e.g. 99.0% MultiArith, 68.2% GPQA Diamond) for the specific datasets used to generate training traces -- no standalone MMLU/MATH/HumanEval. (b) "Qwen-AgentWorld: Language World Models for General Agents" (arxiv.org/html/2606.24597, arXiv:2606.24597) uses Qwen3.5-397B-A17B as a direct baseline row in its main results table (Table 5, "AgentWorldBench"), but that table's columns are agentic-domain scores only (MCP 68.31, Search 30.81, Terminal 55.30, SWE 64.44, Android 54.90, Web 48.55, OS 60.85, Average 54.74) -- no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval columns present anywhere in the paper.

(3) GitHub eval-harness logs: Checked vllm-project/perf-eval (workloads/qwen3_5_h200.yaml configures only a single lm_eval task, gsm8k, 5-shot, for Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-FP8 -- no mmlu/math/humaneval task configured). Checked RedHatAI/Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-FP8-dynamic HF model card, which documents its own independent lm-evaluation-harness re-run (LLM Compressor quantization-recovery methodology) comparing the unquantized 'Original' checkpoint against the FP8-dynamic version across GSM8K-Platinum, MMLU-Pro (Original 88.27 vs FP8 88.33), Math 500 (Original 84.93 vs FP8 84.53), AIME 25 (Original 92.92 vs FP8 94.58), GPQA Diamond (Original 89.73 vs FP8 88.72), IFEval, and LiveCodeBench v6 -- notably this independent third-party re-evaluation of the exact same model also chose MMLU-Pro over plain MMLU and Math 500 over plain MATH, and does not include HumanEval at all, corroborating that the harder successor benchmarks have effectively superseded the legacy ones even in independent re-runs. Checked akitaonrails/llm-coding-benchmark (a custom Rails-API coding rubric scoring the base model 42/100 Tier C -- not HumanEval).

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators: OpenRouter model page (openrouter.ai/qwen/qwen3.5-397b-a17b) lists pricing/context only, no benchmark scores. PapersWithCode search surfaces only an OCRBench entry (931) for this model, no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval leaderboard page exists for it on PapersWithCode. DataLearnerAI category leaderboards surfaced LiveCodeBench and SWE-bench Pro entries only.

(5) Non-English sources: Chinese-language search surfaced only restatements of the official MMLU-Pro/GPQA/AIME26/LiveCodeBench v6 figures already in this record, plus one unverifiable claim (a search-summarizer artifact citing 'mmlu: 87.86%, gpqa diamond: 82.32%, gsm8k: 86.43%, ifeval: 75.90%' attributed to an unnamed 'lm-evaluation-harness run') that could not be traced to any actual page after direct follow-up searches for those exact numbers -- it returned no matching source and appears to conflate/hallucinate from the official MMLU-Pro 87.8 figure, so it was discarded per the no-guessing rule (no real citable sourceUrl). Japanese-language search found the model's Nejumi LLM Leaderboard 4 (wandb-japan) result -- a composite score of 0.8191 (first open model to break the 0.80 barrier, surpassing Gemini-3-Pro-Preview's 0.8134) -- but this is a blended multi-task composite score, not a plain MMLU/MATH/HumanEval breakout; its knowledge-domain subtask is MMLU-ProX (multilingual), not the standard English MMLU already tracked separately in this corpus as a distinct benchmark. An individual Japanese blogger's hands-on review (nowokay.hatenablog.com) contains only qualitative testing, no formal benchmark numbers.

(6) Sibling models: This corpus's own qwen3-5-122b-a10b.json record already documents that AIME/GSM8K/MATH are 'notably absent' from a full line-by-line grep of the same-generation sibling's HF README, and qwen3-6-27b.json (successor dense flagship) likewise has no mmlu/math/humaneval entries. This pass additionally confirms the same absence in the Qwen-AgentWorld paper's baseline comparison table (see item 2b) and in RedHatAI's independent quantization-recovery eval table (see item 3), both of which use Qwen3.5-397B-A17B as a comparison point without ever reporting plain MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval for it.

Conclusion: across all 6 new source-type categories, no independent academic, community, or aggregator source has ever run or reported plain MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval for Qwen3.5-397B-A17B. The pattern is consistent and now doubly corroborated (first pass + this pass): Qwen deliberately retired these three benchmarks for the Qwen3.5 generation in favor of MMLU-Pro/MMLU-Redux, Math500/MathVision/MathVista, and LiveCodeBench v6/SWE-bench Verified, respectively, and every independent re-evaluator found (RedHatAI, Qwen-AgentWorld authors, Compress-Distill authors) followed suit rather than back-filling the legacy numbers. All three gaps reconfirmed as omitted_no_evidence; no new results added.
